questions.q: The Hoysalas are most famous for which achievement?
questions.explanation: The Hoysalas are celebrated for their richly carved stone temples.

questions.q: In which region of the Deccan did the Hoysalas chiefly rule?
questions.explanation: The Hoysalas ruled parts of the southern Deccan, largely in present-day Karnataka.

questions.q: Roughly during which centuries did the Hoysala dynasty flourish?
questions.explanation: The Hoysalas ruled from roughly the 11th to the 14th centuries CE.

questions.q: Which town is a famous site of Hoysala temple architecture?
questions.explanation: Belur, with the Chennakeshava temple, is a celebrated Hoysala site.

questions.q: Hoysala temples are noted for their distinctive ground plan shaped like what?
questions.explanation: Many Hoysala temples have a star-shaped (stellate) plan.

questions.q: Besides Belur, which other town is renowned for its Hoysala temples?
questions.explanation: Halebidu, with the Hoysaleswara temple, is a major Hoysala centre.

questions.q: The Keshava temple at which village is a well-preserved Hoysala monument?
questions.explanation: Somanathapura's Keshava temple is among the finest surviving Hoysala temples.

questions.q: The Hoysala realm declined amid upheavals of which century?
questions.explanation: The Hoysala kingdom fell apart during the turbulent 14th century.

questions.q: Hoysala temples are typically built of which stone, suited to fine carving?
questions.explanation: Hoysala sculptors used soft soapstone, which allowed extraordinarily detailed carving.

questions.q: In which modern Indian state do the major Hoysala temples lie?
questions.explanation: Belur, Halebidu, and Somanathapura are all in the state of Karnataka.

questions.q: What was the principal capital of the Hoysala dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Hoysala capital was Dwarasamudra, today known as Halebidu.

questions.q: The famous Hoysala emblem depicts a warrior fighting which animal, tied to the dynasty's origin legend?
questions.explanation: The Hoysala crest shows the legendary founder Sala striking a lion, the origin of the name 'Hoy-Sala.'

questions.q: Which Hoysala king built the Chennakeshava temple at Belur, traditionally to mark a victory?
questions.explanation: King Vishnuvardhana built the Chennakeshava temple at Belur, commemorating a victory.

questions.q: The Hoysalas began as feudatories of which larger Deccan dynasty before becoming independent?
questions.explanation: The Hoysalas first served as subordinates of the Western Chalukyas of Kalyani.

questions.q: King Vishnuvardhana is associated with conversion to which Vaishnava tradition under the saint Ramanuja's influence?
questions.explanation: Vishnuvardhana, originally a Jain, is said to have embraced Sri Vaishnavism under Ramanuja's influence.

questions.q: Hoysala inscriptions and literature were composed chiefly in which language?
questions.explanation: The Hoysalas patronised Kannada, the dominant language of their realm, alongside Sanskrit.

questions.q: Which Hoysala ruler is regarded as the founder who consolidated early Hoysala power, sometimes called the dynasty's first notable king?
options: Nripa Kama II / the early chiefs leading to independence
questions.explanation: Early Hoysala chiefs such as Nripa Kama II laid the foundations before Vishnuvardhana's expansion.

questions.q: The Hoysaleswara temple at Halebidu is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The twin Hoysaleswara temple at Halebidu is a Shiva temple, while Belur's Chennakeshava honours Vishnu.

questions.q: Hoysala sculptors often signed their work. Where are such artists' signatures typically found on the temples?
questions.explanation: Many Hoysala master sculptors proudly signed their carvings, a rare practice in Indian temple art.

questions.q: Which last great Hoysala ruler fought against the early invasions of the Delhi Sultanate into the south?
questions.explanation: Veera Ballala III, the last major Hoysala king, resisted the Delhi Sultanate's southern campaigns.

questions.q: Several Hoysala temples were inscribed as UNESCO World Heritage Sites in which year?
questions.explanation: The 'Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas' (Belur, Halebidu, Somanathapura) were listed by UNESCO in 2023.

questions.q: Hoysala temples are often called 'dvikuta' or 'trikuta'; what does this describe?
options: Temples with two or three shrines/towers
Temples with two or three moats
Temples for two or three dynasties
Temples lasting two or three centuries
questions.explanation: Dvikuta and trikuta refer to temples having two or three sanctums (and towers) respectively.

questions.q: Which Hoysala general and minister is famed for commissioning the Keshava temple at Somanathapura in 1268 CE?
questions.explanation: The commander Somanatha Dandanayaka built the Somanathapura Keshava temple under Narasimha III.

questions.q: Veera Ballala III died around 1342 fighting the forces of which southern sultanate?
questions.explanation: Veera Ballala III was killed by the Madurai (Ma'bar) Sultanate near Tiruchirapalli around 1342.

questions.q: After the Hoysala collapse, much of their territory was absorbed into which rising empire?
questions.explanation: The newly founded Vijayanagara Empire inherited much of the former Hoysala domain in the mid-14th century.

questions.q: The Chennakeshava temple at Belur is celebrated for its elaborately carved bracket figures of celestial maidens called what?
questions.explanation: Belur's famous bracket sculptures of celestial women are known as madanikas or salabhanjikas.

questions.q: What architectural feature, a raised platform that follows the star-shaped plan, surrounds many Hoysala temples and serves as a circumambulation path?
questions.explanation: Hoysala temples often stand on a raised stellate platform called the jagati for circumambulation.

questions.q: Which Hoysala king moved the dynasty's secondary capital and was active in the Tamil country, intervening among the Pandyas and Cholas?
options: Veera Ballala III (and predecessors operating in the Tamil country)
questions.explanation: Later Hoysalas, notably Veera Ballala III, set up a base at Tiruvannamalai and intervened in Tamil dynastic affairs.

questions.q: The narrative friezes on the lower bands of Hoysala temple walls typically depict scenes from which texts?
questions.explanation: Horizontal friezes on Hoysala temples narrate episodes from the Ramayana, Mahabharata, and Puranas.

questions.q: The Hoysala dynasty's name, by tradition, arose from a command meaning what, spoken by a sage to the founder Sala?
questions.explanation: Legend says a Jain sage cried 'Hoy, Sala!' ('Strike, Sala!') as Sala killed a lion/tiger, giving the dynasty its name.
